chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Const. Petition No. S-553 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objections No.1 to 4 as at ‘A’. 2. For hearing of CMA No.4587 of 2025. 3. For hearing of Main Case. 29.07.2025. M/s. Saathi Ishaq & S.K. Lodhi, advocates for the petitoners. Mr. Muhammad Noonari, Deputy P.G. Sindh along with DSPs (Legal) Raza Mian and DPS Nazimabad Kamal Naeem, Insp./SHO of PS Rizvia Muhammad Ishaq. ********* Through this constitution petition, the petitioners have filed the instant Constitution Petition against sixteen private respondents, as well as the Inspector General of Police, the Senior Superintendent of Police District Central, Karachi and the SHO of Police Station Rizvia with the following prayers as under:- a). To direct the respondents No.1,2,3,4 & 5 and other police officials to produce any further FIRs if lodged in province of Sindh against the petitioners before this Honourable Court except the FIR mentioned in the petition. b). To restrain the respondents No. 4 & 5 and other police officials not to lodge any other FIRs against the petitioner after submission of instant petition without intimation to this Honourable Court. c). To pass any other order which may be deemed just and proper by this Honourable Court giving due regard to the facts and circumstances of the case. 2. The police officials have filed their reports, which are taken on record. It is stated therein that FIR No.242 of 2025 has been lodged against the petitioners under section 406 and 34 PPC and no any other FIR has been reported to or registered at the police station Rizvia. Respondents No.5 to 11, 13, 18, 19 and 21 are present in Court and intend to contest the matter.

It is stated therein that FIR No.242 of 2025 has been lodged against the petitioners under section 406 and 34 PPC and no any other FIR has been reported to or registered at the police station Rizvia. Respondents No.5 to 11, 13, 18, 19 and 21 are present in Court and intend to contest the matter. They contend that they have serious allegations against the present petitioners and for 2 redressal of their grievances, they have given instructions not to proceed with the lodgment of FIRs. 3. Be that as it may, it is very clear from the comments filed on behalf of the SSP District Central, Karachi as well as the SHO of Police Station Rizvia, that apart from the one FIR already lodged, no other FIR has been registered against the petitioners. They have also stated that there is no intention to lodge any further FIR unless a report is formally made. Therefore, the question of harassment does not arise. The lodged FIR has been registered in according with law, the matter has been sent for trial and the case is now fixed for hearing. 4. In such circumstances, the police officials are strictly directed not to cause any harassment to the present petitioners. The lodgment of any further FIR against the petitioners shall be made with prior information to the office of the Inspector General of Police, Sindh. Similarly, the private respondents are at liberty to file any complaint, if they have any grievance against the present petitioners before police station Rizvia. The police officials are directed to address and redress such grievance, if a cognizable offence is made out, strictly in accordance with law. Let a copy of this order be communicated by fax to the Inspector General of Police, Sindh for compliance and further necessary action. 5. The matter is accordingly disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE Manthar Brohi.
